changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security features related to monitoring and identifying unauthorized use. However, some naming improvements were necessary for clarity and consistency. The function names now better reflect the specific checks being performed.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly describe the action or check being performed, such as 'configured' instead of 'enabled' for audit logging.
Use 'logging' in network policy functions to specify the action of capturing unauthorized access attempts.
title: System Monitoring - Identify unauthorized use of the...
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4-b focuses on identifying unauthorized use, which can be mapped to monitoring and logging capabilities in Kubernetes. Audit logging can capture unauthorized access attempts, and admission controllers can enforce policies to monitor such activities. NetworkPolicies can also be configured to log unauthorized network access attempts.
